note: RunPod post-install model VERIFY takes longer than the download itself. Suspect full SHA256 re-read off network volume post-land vs hash-while-streaming. Top of todo (user priority).

summary: Live datapoint 2026-07-23 WEAKENS this card's leading suspect. LTX 2.3 High (61.5GB, our largest weight set) installed to a network volume from a CPU Pod: download 202.7 MB/s, and the VERIFY phase was FAST per the user. The full-SHA256-re-read is real (_sha256_file on the completed .part in wrapper.py) but it just chewed 61.5GB quickly, so per-byte hashing is not the bottleneck. Better hypothesis: per-FILE fixed overhead - ltx-23 High is 12 dependency entries for 61.5GB (~5GB/file) while krea2, the model that felt slow, is 27 entries at a fraction of the bytes. Each file pays an aria2c spawn, an SSE lifecycle, an atomic manifest write onto the volume and a hash open/close, all of which scale with file count not size. Next step: time a Krea2 install on the same Pod and compare seconds-per-FILE, not seconds-per-GB. Detail in research/2026-07-23-ltx-high-verify-datapoint.md.

note: Skipped-Doing auto-correct: real implementation work landed for this card; backfilled todo->doing before done.

summary: Root revised again from the 2026-07-24 live datapoint (Excel model 2GB ControlNet, first file on a cold network volume: download ~1min, verify ~3min). SINGLE file, so per-file overhead is disproven - it is the second full SHA256 READ off the slow network volume (aimdo pins host RAM so no warm page cache). Fix (mpi-ci 04215a2, wrapper 0.2.38): trust aria2 status==complete (per-piece tracking = hole-free) and record app-supplied expected sha as canonical, skipping the re-read; keep full SHA for the RPC-dead belt exit, httpx fallback, and no-expected-sha deps. Pod-only: local/resumable path (downloadManager.js MPI-296) untouched. User-reported verified on live Pod.

note: Install verify near-instant on live Pod (user-verified). Remaining: publish-runtime.sh dev then promote (outward-facing, deferred per MPI-340).
